primary_source: Bateson, Steps to an Ecology of Mind (University of Chicago Press, 1972), ISBN: 978-0226039053; MITRE ATT&CK Enterprise v15 (2024), https://attack.mitre.org.
source_claim: Cert [191] Bateson Learning Levels filtration applied to 691 MITRE ATT&CK techniques via mod-9 encoding should show H1 (dynamics-invariance) coherent with H2 (orbit-family invariance); pre-registered seed + permutation test.
qa_translation: Apply QA orbit-family-modulus filtration (singularity subset family subset modulus subset ambient) to ATT&CK technique indices under mod-9 encoding; test H2 orbit-family invariance by permutation and H1 dynamics preservation by w-statistic with direction declared in pre-registration.
failure_mode: H2 orbit-family invariance p approx 0 — filtration structure confirmed. H1 dynamics w=0.528 with permutation p=0.001 but FLIPPED DIRECTION from pre-registration. NULL verdict declared per pre-reg. Collision artifact suspected in the mod-9 encoding of 691 technique IDs (structural coincidence between technique numbering and QA orbit classes). Hard rule recorded: do not rerun same configuration. Next attempt should use an encoding independent of the MITRE technique ID numbering (e.g. tactic embedding + mod-9 of a different hash).
source_attribution: Will Dale + Claude 2026-04-05; see OB project_bateson_attck_null_with_flip and MEMORY.md hard rule 'never declare QA dead on a null — implementation bug first'
